facts.statement: Cellular respiration converts the chemical energy in nutrients into ATP.
questions: What process converts chemical energy in nutrients into ATP?
How does cellular respiration produce energy?
What is the function of cellular respiration?

facts.statement: Aerobic respiration has three stages: glycolysis, the citric acid cycle, and oxidative phosphorylation.
questions: What are the three stages of aerobic respiration?
How many stages does aerobic respiration have?
What processes make up aerobic respiration?
Where does glycolysis occur?
What is the cellular location of glycolysis?
Which stage of respiration happens in the cytoplasm?

facts.statement: The citric acid cycle occurs in the mitochondria.
questions: Where does the citric acid cycle occur?
What organelle contains the citric acid cycle?
In which cellular compartment does the citric acid cycle take place?

facts.statement: Oxidative phosphorylation occurs in the mitochondria.
questions: Where does oxidative phosphorylation occur?
What is the cellular location of oxidative phosphorylation?
Which organelle houses oxidative phosphorylation?

facts.statement: The complete aerobic respiration of one glucose molecule yields roughly 30 to 32 ATP.
questions: How much ATP does aerobic respiration of glucose produce?
What is the ATP yield from one glucose molecule?
How many ATP molecules are generated from complete glucose oxidation?

facts.statement: Oxygen is the final electron acceptor in the electron transport chain.
questions: What is the final electron acceptor in the electron transport chain?
What role does oxygen play in the electron transport chain?
What molecule accepts electrons at the end of cellular respiration?
